So, this dude named Agent Cavanaugh brings in this other guy, Roybal, for some questioning. See, there's this guy named Chris, and Cavanaugh wants to know when Chris checked out a camera. Then this other guy, Ferris, starts asking Roybal about Chris's look at the camera. Ferris is trying to make Chris unconsciously aware of the dangers that could come his way.

Then Roybal finds out that the dude Chris tackled actually shot up two other casinos! Can you believe it? And guess what? Roybal realizes he has this weird ability too.

So yeah, that's what went down. Pretty wild, huh?
what's the difference between glance, look, gaze? explain like i'm 5

Sure, I'll explain it to you like you're 5!

Imagine you're sitting in your room playing with your toys, and you quickly turn your head to take a quick peek at something that caught your attention. That quick peek is a glance! It's when you quickly look at something and then look away.

Now, let's say you're sitting at the dinner table and your mom asks you where you put your favorite toy. You might take a moment to really think about it. You might turn your head and focus your eyes on a specific spot to search for the toy. That's called looking! It's when you focus your eyes on something for a bit longer, like searching or trying to find something.

Lastly, let's say you're at the park and you see a squirrel climbing up a tree. You might become really fascinated and keep your eyes locked onto the squirrel as it moves. That's called gazing! It's when you look at something for a longer period of time, like when you're really interested or captivated by it.

So, glance is like a quick look, look is like searching or finding something, and gaze is like staring with your eyes fixated on something. Pretty cool, right?

He obviously went to the diner many times at the certain time to see her, but there was no sign of her.

So, Chris goes back to his humble home, this old garage where he stays with an old man. They're having a good time, playing a friendly game of pool. As they're hanging out, Chris starts talking about this girl who's been on his mind a lot. He mentions how she always shows up at this diner at exactly 8:09.

Chris tells the old man that he can actually see into the future for this girl, but only a little bit. He can kinda predict what's gonna happen to her beyond just a couple of minutes. So, you know what he does? He keeps going to that diner over and over at the same time, hoping to see her. But every time, no sign of her.

Hope that makes it easier for you to understand!

A little while later, Chris predicts that Ferris will come by. He thinks about whether he should stick around and have a chat with her. But then he realizes something - Ferris actually knows about his special ability, and she's gonna ask him to help find a stolen nuclear thingy in return for ignoring the fact that he stole a car. Sneaky, huh?

But here's the thing, Chris also sees into the future that he's gonna get arrested if he gets involved. So, what does he do? He decides to split before Ferris even shows up.

Pretty smart move, if you ask me!

Then it hits him, Ferris actually knows about his superpower and she's gonna ask him to help find a stolen nuke. Crazy, right? Smart move, right?
10 phrases for when a man stares too much at a woman and looks at her from head to toe with definition

1. Checking her out: When a man looks at a woman from head to toe, examining her in a way that suggests he finds her attractive or appealing.

2. Giving her the once-over: When a man quickly looks at a woman's entire appearance, often in a way that can make her feel uncomfortable or objectified.

3. Eyeing her up and down: When a man openly and noticeably looks at a woman's body, starting from her face and moving downwards.

4. Ogling: When a man stares at a woman with intense interest, typically focusing on her physical features or body.

5. Leering: When a man looks at a woman in a way that is creepy or suggestive, often accompanied by a suggestive smile or inappropriate gestures.

6. Gawking: When a man openly stares at a woman for a prolonged period, either out of curiosity or because he finds her physically appealing.

7. Scanning her from head to toe: When a man visually examines a woman's entire body, usually in an intense or deliberate manner.

8. Indecently staring: When a man stares at a woman in a way that is inappropriate, offensive, or disrespectful.

9. Pervy gaze: When a man's gaze appears lustful or suggestive, making the woman feel uncomfortable or objectified.

10. Creepy staring: When a man looks at a woman in a way that makes her feel uneasy or gives her the impression that he may have ulterior motives.
